brand.name: Khushi Baby Association
brand.tagline: Making public health data meaningful, connected and actionable
brand.lede: We partner with Government Health Departments in India to close the feedback loop between data collection and coordinated care

quote.title: Public health systems cannot respond to needs they can't see
quote.body: Across health systems, vast amounts of data are collected every day without translating into timely decisions or meaningful action. Information remains fragmented across programs and platforms, leaving frontline health workers overburdened and decision makers without clear insights into what is happening on the ground.

diaLead: We are strengthening the public health system by closing the gap between data, insights and action — to enable timely and critical health services to the most vulnerable.

depthReach.title: We are closely working with government bodies in Rajasthan, Maharashtra and Karnataka across primary health care.
depthReach.sideNote: OUR DIGITAL PLATFORMS SUPPORT 80,000+ COMMUNITY HEALTH WORKERS, AND REACH 60 MILLION+ PEOPLE ACROSS THREE STATES.

inhouse.p1: We run an in-house multidisciplinary team of tech, data science, programs, policy and implementation.
inhouse.p2: This makes it possible for us to work with stakeholders across the healthcare system and close the missing feedback loop of healthcare to all communities.

hiring.title: We're hiring specialists who are deeply invested in strengthening public health in India

next3.p1: The next three years for Khushi Baby is focused around scaling deep.
next3.p2: We are closely working with every stakeholder in the State healthcare ecosystem to ensure the data collected is carefully analysed by smart tech and leveraged to rapidly make tactical actions.

chipStats.label: ADDED THROUGH FIRST-TIME CENSUS IN 5 NEW DISTRICTS IN 2025
Multi-dimensional social-health vulnerability mapped at village level

dia.title: DATA
dia.body: Our digital platforms make data collection simple and free of duplication for frontline health workers. Information captured on the ground is consolidated and made visible to the right stakeholders in the system.

dia.title: INSIGHTS
dia.body: Our analytical infrastructure converts raw data into clear, decision-ready insights that are presented in ways that make action straightforward for everyone in the system.

dia.title: ACTION
dia.body: Our health action centres enable data-driven decisions and improved health services to close the loop of healthcare to the last-mile communities. This can come from a change in policy, program, practice or partnership.

caseStudies.title: Data driven maternal and child health care
Closing the child nutrition care continuum

testimonials.quote: Khushi Baby has tested, scaled up a model, which is fantastic. It's integrated, it's providing a lot of efficiency. What I like about what KB has done is they haven't actually tried to push a digital solution in. They have tried to solve a real problem by using digital. And that is exactly what needs to be done. Look at the problem. If it needs a digital intervention, yes, but let's not just take any bright, shiny object and push it in.
testimonials.name: NEERAJ JAIN
testimonials.role: Director Growth Operations, Asia, Middle East & Europe (AMEE), PATH

testimonials.quote: All the apps available in the open market are created by software engineers. Why these were not successfully implemented is because the needs of the healthcare person is different. Khushi Baby had an advantage over it because they have public health experts in their system, they have computer experts in their system so understanding what the government, what the community want can be easily translated to the software expert.

howItWorks.body: Every deployment begins by sitting with district and state officials to identify the most pressing health priority — whether maternal mortality, child malnutrition, TB, immunisation gaps, or another program. We establish a baseline, identify where the care cascade breaks, and define what closing the loop looks like here. This is not a rapid assessment. It takes time, field visits, and co-design with community health workers — who understand the barriers to care that no dashboard will ever surface on its own. The question we start with is: what needs to happen to solve the problem.

howItWorks.eyebrow: Building the Infrastructure that Enables Solutions
howItWorks.body: Before a single data point is collected, we work with government counterparts to agree on entry and exit strategies, operationalize MoU terms, and integrate with existing digital portals. A clear mandate to reduce duplicate data entry for health workers — not add to it — is a non-negotiable starting condition. This means training and supporting community health workers and officials, developing government budget lines for long-term ownership, and ensuring the digital infrastructure serves people rather than fragmenting their work further. The enabling environment is as important as the platform itself.

Community health workers use CHIP to enumerate every household across the geography — establishing a live registry of named, located individuals that every health program can track over time. This is the substrate on which all insights and actions depend. Without it, targeting is directional at best. The census maps multi-dimensional vulnerability — social, economic, geographic, and health — and integrates with national platforms so data collected once flows across programs rather than being re-entered multiple times.

Raw data becomes actionable through AI and GIS dashboards that show where high-risk populations are concentrated, where referral loops are breaking down, and which individuals need follow-up before their window closes. Insights flow to officials at block, district, and state level — including health secretary and chief minister offices — and are directly linked to action scheduling. Supportive supervision structures for community health workers, live data quality monitoring, and an inbound and outbound call center ensure the system doesn't stall at the insights layer.

In districts identified by NITI Aayog and government as top-priority and aspirational geographies, Khushi Baby co-establishes a Health Action Center with the health department. A lean interdisciplinary unit is proximally deployed to establish a command center and prioritize closing the public health feedback loop — building on our COVID-19 pandemic response experience and mimicking the urgency of those war rooms. The HAC does not replace government health workers. It coordinates them. Care escalations, referral follow-ups, and daily outreach that would otherwise fall through the cracks are tracked, acted on, and measured.

Closing the loop at the district level creates evidence and tools that travel further — informing policy at state and national level, enabling partner organizations to reach communities Khushi Baby cannot, and building a continuously improving evidence base for what works in public health.

subItems.title: Scale deep
subItems.body: Vertical technologies — smartphone diagnostics, LLM-powered tools, AI triage — are tested at scale within CHIP, then made available as government-owned infrastructure for partners to build on.

subItems.title: Evidence
subItems.body: Our data layer creates a continuously updated evidence base for public health interventions — shared with research partners, informing program design, and feeding back into how we operate.

subItems.title: Policy
subItems.body: CHIP data has shaped national TB surveillance strategy, Maharashtra's tribal health policy, and district action plans for climate-health vulnerability — turning ground-level evidence into systemic change.

subItems.title: Open platform
subItems.sub: Shared for others to build on
subItems.body: Playbooks, datasets, and platform components are open-sourced — so partner NGOs and governments can deploy proven solutions without starting from scratch, and reach deeper into communities we cannot directly serve.

chipLayers.body: Community health workers use CHIP to perform a digital health census of their village, track health longitudinally across programs, and measure health service delivery progress in a unified interface. CHIP reduces over 180 redundant indicators over 12 national health programs.

CHIP empowers health officials with AI and GIS-based dashboards providing real-time monitoring of community needs and health system performance across multiple health programs. Geospatial mapping makes it possible to see where high-risk populations are concentrated, where service delivery gaps exist, and where targeted action is most urgently needed. These insights flow to health officials at the block, district, and state level, enabling data-informed decision-making.

CHIP enables longitudinal follow-up across health areas, ensuring individuals identified as high-risk are tracked throughout their full care journey and not lost between visits, referrals, or departments. The dashboard enables health officials to directly link insights to actions by prompting users to schedule public health outreach interventions and initiating an automated impact assessment.

chipUnique: Positioned as a digital public good to be owned, adapted, and scaled by government health departments
A unified interface across health workers and national health programs.
Co-designed with community health workers and built for the last mile.
Demonstrated 12% improvement in infant immunization rates through automated follow-up mechanisms
Scaled to 85K community health workers across cadres (ASHAs, ANMs, CHOs), across three states, reaching 60M people
hacSteps: HACs get set up by KB team, Health Dept. and local partner
Periodic review, iterative learning and DIA adaptation

nandurbar.title: Nandurbar Health Action Center
nandurbar.intro: Nandurbar sits in northwestern Maharashtra — dense forests, hilly terrain, deeply rooted tribal communities with a 69% Scheduled Tribe population. The Maternal mortality is 4× the state average.
nandurbar.desc: In Nandurbar, we set up a six-person embedded team operating from the district administrative headquarters — program manager, data analyst, four nurses as call center operators — that turns routine health data into real-time action — ensuring no high-risk mother or child falls through the cracks.

panels.body: Nandurbar sits in northwestern Maharashtra — dense forests, hilly terrain, deeply rooted tribal communities with a 69% Scheduled Tribe population. The Maternal mortality is 4× the state average. In Nandurbar, we set up a six-person embedded team operating from the district administrative headquarters — program manager, data analyst, four nurses as call center operators — that turns routine health data into real-time action — ensuring no high-risk mother or child falls through the cracks.

The CHIP digital health census surveys 87.7% of 1.8M residents. It integrates 47+ datasets: WhatsApp group intelligence from CHWs, SNCU discharge lists, health worker high-risk nominations, ad hoc surveys. Every household is GPS-tagged. 2,085 ASHAs are digitally enabled on e-SUCHI.

panels.sub: Surface where the gaps are — in real time
panels.body: Our integrated dashboard with 40+ layers identifies which PHCs reported zero high-risk pregnancies despite 1,591 estimated cases. Prioritized triage lists tell each worker exactly who to visit. Multi-dimensional vulnerability maps are shared directly with the District Collector.

Four call center operators follow up with 500+ highest-risk mothers and children weekly. Field teams coordinate home visits, emergency transport, and focused investigations. Weekly government reviews convert gaps into assigned, time-bound actions.

closingLoop.body: HAC Associates identified 3 high-risk newborns in Akkalkuwa, Dhadgaon, and Taloda blocks who had not received mandated home-based newborn care visits. Through 1–2 targeted follow-ups and coordination with frontline workers, all three were converted to compliance. Two low-birth-weight children showed significant weight gain. In one case, the child's weight was recorded for the first time since birth.

teleHAC.title: Tele-HAC: Scaling the Action Layer for Community Outreach
teleHAC.body: The current HAC model is powerful but human-intensive. TeleHAC is a forthcoming AI-augmented module within the CHIP dashboard that transforms the manual call center operation into a scalable, quality-monitored community outreach system. Where a manual call center is constrained by operator bandwidth and supervision capacity, TeleHAC brings in automated triage prioritization — surfacing the highest-risk beneficiaries for next follow-up — structured counseling support for operators, and longitudinal tracking of conversation quality. The goal is to extend the reach and depth of community follow-up without proportionally scaling the team size, and to generate the kind of systematic learning about what counseling approaches work that a manual system cannot produce.

about.heroSub: We are committed to empowering community health workers and public health decision makers with innovative, data-driven solutions
about.mission: To strengthen last mile health systems by empowering community health workers and partners with digital tools for bridging gaps in primary health care.
about.vision: A resilient, data-driven health system in which every child, family and village is supported to achieve happiness and well-being — in other words, khushi.
about.story: In his junior year, Ruchit took the course 'Appropriate Technology for the Developing World' — taught by Professors Joseph Zinter and Robert Hopkins at the Yale Center for Engineering Innovation and Design. The objective of the course was to develop innovations to address the world's immunization gap. Ruchit and his team focused on the area of data and accountability, specifically for creation of a digital vaccine record.
about.teamIntro: Our 90-member team is at the forefront of creating digital health platforms that enhance accessibility, efficiency, and effectiveness, ensuring that quality healthcare reaches the last mile.

values.title: What Drives Us
values.subtitle: We partner, build, and learn with the communities we serve. Our team speaks to or visits with 100–500 community health workers every day. Together we have spent 250K+ hours in the field.

items.title: Technology
items.body: We build innovative, integrated digital health solutions co-designed with frontline health workers through 250,000+ hours of collaboration.

items.title: Team
items.body: Our multidisciplinary team combines tech, data science, programs, policy, and field implementation — hired from the communities they serve.

items.title: Tradition
items.body: We respect and build upon existing health systems and local knowledge, ensuring solutions are culturally grounded and sustainable.

approach.title: Digital Platforms
approach.body: We develop unified digital health solutions, co-designed with over 250,000 hours of working alongside frontline health workers.

approach.title: Health System Strengthening
approach.body: We support public health decision-makers with data-driven insights and policies to close health equity gaps.

approach.title: Research & Development
approach.body: We collaborate with experts to deploy AI/ML technologies for precision-public health delivery and impact evaluation.

impact.heroSub: Maximizing impact is at the core of our work. Impact at Khushi Baby is created through data-driven interventions and measured systematically through an iterative process.

healthAreas.title: Maternal Health
healthAreas.body: Improving prenatal care adherence, access to high-risk pregnancy tracking, and unmet family planning needs through digital tools and machine learning prioritization.

healthAreas.title: Child Malnutrition
healthAreas.body: High Risk Child Tracking program integrating digital health tools to facilitate a closed referral loop — quality data collection, automated flagging, and field verification to dramatically improve detection of malnutrition.

healthAreas.title: Immunization
healthAreas.body: Demonstrated 12% improvement in infant immunization rates through automated follow-up mechanisms and real-time monitoring of vaccine rollouts.

healthAreas.title: Tuberculosis
healthAreas.body: Screening vulnerable populations for TB, shaping national TB surveillance strategy, and integrating with Ni-kshay national TB registry.

healthAreas.title: Non-Communicable Diseases
healthAreas.body: Beneficiaries screened for NCDs and substance use, enabling early detection and identifying health disparities across communities.

healthAreas.title: Climate & Health
healthAreas.body: Identified blocks in drought-prone, heat wave-prone, and poor air quality areas. Built India's first village-level multidimensional poverty index.

impact.tocBody: Khushi Baby's solution includes team + technology for both health systems strengthening and measurement of the health system's impact. Creating an impact in public health is a multi-step, multi-stakeholder, and multi-year process. Measuring impact requires first measuring the accuracy of reported data and establishing a mixed-methods process for longitudinal evaluation and independent validation.

tocFormula.title: Team
tocFormula.body: Capacity building support for end-to-end platform implementation and data-driven decision-making.

tocFormula.title: Tech
tocFormula.body: CHIP platform harmonizes 800+ public health indicators across 12 national health programs.

tocFormula.title: Tradition
tocFormula.body: Solutions designed to measure bio-psycho-social, climate, and gender related frameworks for public health impact.

fellowship.title: KB Fellowship Program
fellowship.body: We offer a comprehensive fellowship experience that provides hands-on learning, professional development, and a deep dive into our innovative approach to healthcare technology. Founded out of Yale in 2014, Khushi Baby partners with Harvard and Yale students to get involved in community health work spanning 11 SDG goals.
areas: Disease Modeling and Big Data Analytics

donate.title: Support Our Mission
donate.body: Your contribution helps us strengthen last-mile health systems and empower community health workers across India. Every donation directly supports our work in reaching the most vulnerable communities.
